---
title: "Contributing to crossLagR (internal)"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Contributing to crossLagR (internal)}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::knitr}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment  = "#>",
  eval     = FALSE
)
```

> **This document is for contributors, not end users.** It is excluded from
> the built package via `.Rbuildignore`, so it does not appear in
> `vignette()` or on CRAN. Read this on GitHub before you open a PR.

## What this package is

`crossLagR` is a set of lavaan-style wrappers for panel-data SEM. Every
estimator emits lavaan syntax with **unified labels** (`ar_x`, `ar_y`,
`cl_xy`, `cl_yx`, ...) following Usami, Murayama & Hamaker (2019). The
diagnostic stack (`lavaanDiagnose`, `crossLagRSummary`, `diagnoseFit`,
`iccReport`) is built around those labels.

Treat unified labels as a contract --- if you add an estimator, it must
emit `ar_x`/`ar_y`/`cl_xy`/`cl_yx` wherever those concepts exist, or
nothing downstream (`crossLagRSummary`, `monteCarloLavaan`,
`simFromSyntax`) will work for it.

## Repository layout

```
crossLagR/
├── R/              # package source. Each estimator in its own file.
├── tests/testthat/ # unit + integration tests. One file per public fn.
├── man/            # roxygen-generated .Rd (don't edit by hand).
├── NAMESPACE       # hand-maintained; uses exportPattern.
├── DESCRIPTION     # package metadata, dependencies.
├── inst/shinySim/  # bundled Shiny simulator (launch_shiny_sim()).
├── vignettes/      # user-facing vignettes (Rmd).
├── quarto_book/    # user guide, rendered to GitHub Pages.
└── .github/        # CI workflows (test, pkgdown + Quarto deploy).
```

`R/zzz.R` holds the `.onAttach()` BETA-status startup message. Bump or
remove when promoting beyond BETA.

## First-time setup

Clone and open in RStudio:

```{bash, eval = FALSE}
git clone https://github.com/crweber9874/crossLagR.git
cd crossLagR
```

The project uses **renv** for dependency pinning. From R:

```r
install.packages("renv")
renv::restore()       # installs the exact deps from renv.lock
```

If you don't want renv, install the development tools by hand:

```r
install.packages(c("devtools", "testthat", "roxygen2",
                   "lavaan", "dplyr", "tidyr", "ggplot2"))
```

Load the package without installing:

```r
devtools::load_all()
```

You should see the BETA startup banner. If you don't, you are looking at an
installed copy instead of the source tree.

## Run a simple model

The whole package fits in three steps:

```r
library(crossLagR)

# 1. Simulate data from a known DGP
dat <- simCLPM(waves = 4, sample_size = 500, seed = 1)$data

# 2. Build syntax + fit with full diagnostics
out <- lavaanDiagnose(
  estimateCLPM(waves = 4),
  data         = dat,
  meanstructure = TRUE,
  icc_vars     = c("x", "y")
)

# 3. Inspect
out                  # full bundled report (summary + diagnosis + ICC)
out$summary          # just the labelled parameter table
out$diagnosis        # just Heywood-case diagnostics
out$icc              # just the ICC report
```

For a Monte Carlo sweep:

```r
grid <- data.frame(
  stability_p = 0.3, stability_q = 0.3,
  cross_p = 0.1,   cross_q = 0.15,
  variance_p = 0.5, variance_q = 0.5,
  variance_between_x = 0.5, variance_between_y = 0.5,
  cov_pq = 0
)
mc <- monteCarloLavaan(estimator = "CLPM", dgp = "RICLPM",
                       trials = 50, waves = 4, sample_size = 500,
                       param_grid = grid)
```

## Run the tests

After any change to `R/`:

```r
devtools::test()             # full suite
devtools::test_active_file() # just the test file currently open
```

Conventions:

- Every new public function gets a `tests/testthat/test-<fn>.R`.
- Tests that fit lavaan use `skip_on_cran()` and `skip_if_not_installed("lavaan")`.
- Always assert `n_obs` / `n_miss` are reported (CLAUDE.md preference).
- Don't assert exact parameter recovery --- use tolerances (`expect_lt(abs(est - truth), 0.15)`).
- For new sim/estimator combos, use small N and few trials so the suite stays CI-friendly.

## Document new code

Roxygen comments above the function:

```r
#' @title myFunction
#' @description One-line summary.
#' @param x Numeric.
#' @return A data.frame.
#' @export
myFunction <- function(x) { ... }
```

Then regenerate the `.Rd` files:

```r
devtools::document()
```

**Do not hand-edit anything in `man/`.** `NAMESPACE` is hand-maintained
(uses `exportPattern("^[[:alpha:]]+")`) so new exported functions don't
require a NAMESPACE edit --- but check that your function name starts with
a letter.

## Add a new estimator

If your estimator follows the Usami et al. (2019) unified framework, it
must:

1. Emit lavaan syntax with the standard labels: `ar_x`, `ar_y`, `cl_xy`,
   `cl_yx` on the dynamic equation; `d_var_x`, `d_var_y`, `d_cov_xy` on
   dynamic residuals. Model-specific labels (`I_x`, `S_x`, etc.) should
   match the table in `unifiedLabels()`.
2. Take `waves` as its first argument.
3. Return a character string (not a fitted object).
4. Have tests in `tests/testthat/test-estimateMyModel.R` covering:
   syntax contents, lavaan parsing (`lavaan::lavaanify()`), and a small
   end-to-end fit on simulated data.

If your estimator introduces a new unified label, add it to
`.UNIFIED_LABEL_TABLE` in `R/crossLagRSummary.R` with a one-line
description and a group.

## Build the book

```bash
cd quarto_book
quarto render            # full book
quarto render A_package_reference.qmd --to html   # single chapter
```

Output lands in `quarto_book/_book/`. The GitHub Action in
`.github/workflows/pages.yaml` rebuilds and deploys on every push to
`master`.

## Commit conventions

- Imperative subject lines: "Add X", "Fix Y", "Refactor Z" --- not "Added"
  or "Fixing".
- Keep subject under ~70 characters; expand in the body.
- Group related changes into one commit. Don't bundle unrelated work.
- Never commit `.rda` files (CLAUDE.md rule).
- Never push without explicit authorization on this repo.

## Reporting bugs

Open an issue at
<https://github.com/crweber9874/crossLagR/issues> with:

- the function call that triggered the problem,
- `sessionInfo()`,
- a minimal reproducible example (small N, few waves, fixed seed),
- the actual vs expected behavior.

For Heywood-case questions, run `heywoodHelp()` first --- the catalog may
already explain what you are seeing.

## Style

- tidyverse-flavored R (`dplyr`, `tidyr`, pipes), but the package keeps
  dependencies thin --- prefer base R if a tidyverse import would be the
  only use.
- Minimal commenting (CLAUDE.md). Comment the *why*, not the *what*.
- Prefer Bayesian / `brms` / `blavaan` over frequentist where it doesn't
  fight existing code.
- Report `n_obs` and `n_miss` alongside any estimate (see how
  `crossLagRSummary` does it).
- Cite sources when claiming a fact about a dataset (codebook, paper,
  README). The book bibliography lives at `quarto_book/book.bib`.
